review_title: Easy to use and help us to automate our standard work.
description: Easy to use program and have multiple option manage difrent hardware takes care of patching backups and Security (sentinelOne) Scripts are easy to use and to add. It takes allot of standard work out of our hands so that we can focus on the more fun stuff.
author: Olaf V.
pros: Easy to use and to configurate. Single pain of glass. so i can see everything i need and use. Takes allot of standard work out of our hands.
cons: There are so many extra options that it is sometimes hard to decide what we will add.

description: Overall, super happy and impressed. We tried both N-Able and Ninja, and it was a clear winner. Their support has been great, and our account executive is also really knowledgeable. If we can't figure it out via the dojo docs, we've always been given clear instructions via their support team.
author: Eric K.
pros: Overall, the user interface is clean, informative and modern. You are able to easily navigate between organizations. Profile management is easy and allows you to categorize systems by location (and apply a policy at a location level). Having a single pane of glass to review each morning saves a significant amount of time and allows me to be proactive in letting my end users know where troubles are, rather than having them report - helps show the value of purchasing the services from me.
cons: The backup features are getting better, but there are still some rough patches (for us at least). I'd like the opportunity to back up to a cloud provider (and storage target of my choice), as well as the ability to back up hardware devices. I have a few clients that have NAS's on their network, however I really don't have a great way of backing them up (apart from replicating the data to a local machine). This works for some of the smaller data stores, but not the larger ones.

review_title: NinjaOne - Customer service Nightmare and total money grab
description: We have been using NinjaOne since 2020. While the software served as a decent starting RMM, it often fell short of the promises made, such as the integration with Bitdefender GravityZone and market-comparable pricing for backup solutions. Additionally, it took over three months to discontinue services that were no longer available as add-ons within the portal. As an MSP, we experienced fluctuations in our monthly usage. Despite being sold on a per-seat model, the subscription-based contract required us to notify them five days in advance of any changes. On January 10th, I informed [sensitive content hidden], our account manager, via email and phone that we would be transitioning to ConnectWise. I emphasized the need to avoid penalties due to the five-day notification requirement. By March, NinjaOne expressed surprise and delayed the transition by almost three weeks, attempting to extend it beyond the five-day period to charge us for endpoints we no longer had installed. Despite having zero endpoints, they claimed we did not provide a 60-day notice. I advise caution when dealing with NinjaOne, especially if your account manager is Jake Hunter. Ensure all communications are documented, as reducing or terminating services can be challenging.
author: Robert S.
pros: Screen share/connect software runs smooth and efficient
cons: Mobile support Billing Account Manager Reporting Automation anything Scripts fail all the time

description: It is a powerful, modern RMM tool. It has significantly improved our team's efficiency by automating routine maintenance and remote support.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The interface is very fast and easy to navigate. It allows me to manage and monitor all my endpoints from a single, clean dashboard without any lag.
cons: The reporting tools can be a bit limited for complex needs. I wish there were more built-in templates to customize data for my clients.

description: NinjaOne has provided a reliable and user-friendly solution for managing systems. The dashboard is clean, reporting is effective, and deployment is straightforward. It has streamlined IT operations and reduced overhead.
author: Steve B.
pros: The product offers a seamless deployment process. Easily deployable with minimal effort and Deploy in minutes, not hours
cons: The solution should provide expanded support for on-premises backups. The current backup solution would benefit from additional on-premises backup options

description: Overall, my experience with NinjaOne has been exceptional. From the considerate account managers, to the devoted development team who actually cares about feedback on how to improve the platform. There has not been a moment we have considerate an alternate RMM solution since we adopted NinjaOne, and I don't anticipate that changing any time soon.
author: Lorenzo K.
pros: NinjaOne's dedication to improvements, engagement with their community, and strong automation capabilities all make this platform a no brainer to me. On top of these, the interface is extremely user friendly for new users, and issues are rarely present on the platform itself.
cons: Customer support has been declining in quality over the last few years. Organizational features still are not quite where we'd like them, in that it would be nice to have folder structures for things like policies, reports, etc.

description: We migrated to NinjaOne from CW Automate and have not looked back in any capacity. The platform is well designed and functions well. It is our most critical line of business application for supporting our clients and we are very happy with the solution.
author: Peter A.
pros: The UI and dashboard is simple, clean, and effective. They have feature parity and then some across their RMM and remote software that allows our team to manage a significant amount of endpoints with ease. It is an extremely effective product with a smart team behind it, and the cost is very competitive.
cons: There is the odd bug or change introduced that hinders operations, but it's generally minimal and their support and development teams are excellent in troubleshooting and resolving issues.

review_title: NinjaOne - Helped Us Manage Remote Locations
description: Great! It helps me and my team manage the health of remote machines, administer software, fix issues, track/log problems, and report on the overall well-being of the company's infrastructure.
author: Joe K.
pros: Ease of use, all of the functionality it offers, allows me to manage multiple sites for customer support, the dashboard allows me to identify and address problems promptly, and ticketing helps log and track end users primary issues.
cons: Too in-depth, you can really get into the nitty-gritty of it, and it can be a bit intimidating seeing all the options before you.

review_title: One hell of a RMM SaaS utility
author: Nathan C.
pros: Pricing, Support, Ease of Use, Platform View, Management. The NinjaOne Platform has been the easiest RMM utility I've used in the past few years. We've switched from another provider for archaic, old layouts and limited scripting abilities.
cons: Support help area is hidden behind SSO. Some features may be "too simplified" or "too easy to use" that blocks the full features.

description: My overall experience has been great with NinjaOne. It has off-boarded multiple tools in our environment and it has streamlined our ability to support our systems.
author: Allen F.
pros: Customer support is great but the community around the tool is even greater. I enjoy the dashboard and how many tools they've added into the platform. It has been extremely easy to remotely manage any computer added to the platform.
cons: I have struggled with the Apple integration a bit. We have a bit of a unique setup on our MacBooks but NinjaOne should be able to handle it still.

description: Overall excellent experience. I can't recommend them enough. The people are great, support is knowledgeable and responsive. Software works well. Pricing is reasonable. They're continuing to add features and communicate with its partners. They really seem to have it together. Onboarding was a breeze. Their documentation is extensive and many of the articles include explainer videos. Easy to use.
author: Asher O.
pros: The people at NinjaOne are great. They're big enough where they have the benefits of a large organizations, but small enough where I feel like I have fast, personalized support. Everyone from the initial salesperson to account manager and technicians have been very professional and responsive. Their software just works. I rarely have issues with the managed agents. If I ever have a question, their support gets back to me the same day, sometimes the same hour. Very impressive!
cons: I'm hard-pressed to say anything bad, as nearly everything is great. There are a few minor improvements that could be made, but more of a nice-to-have than a need. Even so, what I love is that they have a publicly visible product roadmap so you can see what's coming next.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Why did we not try them out sooner
description: After trialing the product we are in the process of putting together a schedule to migrate from our existing solution to NinjaOne
author: Joe H.
pros: The interface is amazing and feature packed. Also support is very helpfull and informative while setting up and testing the product
cons: It can become a little pricey if you have many users or a low number of users but very endpoint heavy.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: All round seems like a good system. No complaints
author: Laura C.
pros: I like the remote login so that it can log in
cons: No complaints as of yet. Not been using it long
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A solid, simple, modern RMM solution
description: It's simple and doesn't represent a huge investment of time, which when you have a lot on your plate is optimal.
author: Eliot F.
pros: We recently migrated to NinjaRMM and I'm really impressed how simple and straightforward it is to set up. It also has a really clean, modern UI which is a big plus compared to some of the more legacy players in the market. Another big plus is that it supports disabling/hiding a lot of features that you don't want to use, which makes it easier to do a phased rollout/implementation. Lastly Ninja's onboarding services were good and their techs were helpful!
cons: Since it is a newer player on the scene, there's a lot of rapid development going on, so some things you'd expect from a more established product might be not-exactly-how-you'd-expect-it, and you're waiting on their (tbf, pretty rapid) development cadence to catch up.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Our overall experience of NinjaRMM is massively positive. The platform has enabled us as an MSP to fully manage all our clients patching and asset management needs.
author: Nick R.
pros: Excellent portal interface, that is so clear and concise for everyone to use. The patch management and asset information is top notch. The features and functionality of the platform are also superb and always being added to.
cons: Nothing, the platform works so much better than the RMM software we replaced.

author: David C.
pros: Having a central pane of glass for all of my network management needs is fantastic. I am able to control patches, remote control user PCs, and deploy software seamlessly.
cons: We are looking to have a full mobile device management capability with conditional access to completely lock down our environment.

review_title: Easy to Use and Reliable RMM
description: Overall, NinjaOne has been a really solid RMM tool. It saves a lot of time on day-to-day tasks and makes it easy to keep everything updated and healthy. It feels reliable, and it’s taken a lot of stress out of managing multiple systems.
author: Matthew C.
pros: I really like how simple NinjaOne is to use. The interface is clean, and it doesn’t take long to get the hang of it. The automation is a big time-saver — patching, scripts, and software installs just run smoothly in the background. I also appreciate how stable the remote access is, and their support team is genuinely helpful when you need them.
cons: The reporting side could be better. I wish there were more options to customize reports, especially for clients who want something branded or a bit more detailed. The mobile app works, but it’s definitely not as full-featured as the desktop version.

description: I've used NinjaOne for multiple companies now. They are a great RMM most of the features you need for managing and configuring all your devices. I was overall very happy with it.
author: Justin G.
pros: Ease of use. The UI is very friendly and makes using the product a joy. It is very feature fledged, with more features on the way.
cons: There were times were some features were slightly glitchy. Support was helpful to troubleshooting and escalating issues.

review_title: Efficient and User-Friendly IT Management Tool
description: After the initial confusion with the pricing, managing and monitoring with NinjaOne has been very nice.
author: Heldor G.
pros: It has amazing automation features with a very user-friendly interface, making remote monitoring and management easy.
cons: The pricing structure can be confusing, and some advanced reporting features could be more customizable.

author: Aiden A.
pros: Relatively expensive, especially for a small team. This seems to kind of conflict with the actual use case of ninja one, for small to medium teams. That being said, setting up management devices is extremely straight forward and saved a lot of time.
cons: It is good at what it is good at, advanced features for automation and analytics is really difficult to properly accomplish. The learning curve is exponential, starting out simple.

review_title: NinjaOne keeps me organized and on top of my tasks effortlessly. It's a game-changer for productivity!
author: Laura T.
pros: Seamless task management and intuitive interface make it a go-to for staying on track.
cons: The reporting features could be more robust, and I'd like to see more integrations with other apps.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Great RMM, but expensive and lacks some features
description: The company I worked at used NinjaOne for a while, and it was great, however, it lacked features that aren't critical, but nice to have. It was also quite a bit more expensive than some other RMM solutions.
author: Kevin C.
pros: NinjaOne is extremely easy to use, has robust automation features, responsive customer support, and comprehensive remote management.
cons: Some cons of NinjaOne are its limited integration options, feature gaps, and it's a little pricey.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Erik H.
pros: Quickdeployment, easy to use, quick response on support questions, good boarding policy
cons: Pricing not direct available only via accountmanager
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Lewis M.
pros: I love the features, specifically the fact that you can do ad hoc connections and users do not need to be on the domain.
cons: Setting up was difficult, also difficult to remove and reinstall.

review_title: Ninjaone makes IT more efficient and meets our requirements
description: NinjaOne is a good experience, with great staff, and good prices - always changing and evolving products.With its ability to support a large number of computers while I am in my office, and its ability to proactively and reliably manage the infrastructure of our client's network, I consider it my right hand in my dealings with my clients and it has saved me the time to go to the client site, which I invested by adding more clients.Most importantly, I am a fan of working from home and social distancing to prevent some diseases.as well as the automation of the program Routine operations, are carried out without intrusiveness, which greatly improves work efficiencyNinja One customer support is provided by the help desk where they handle problems and help me reach my goal of serving my customers.
author: Ghassan C.
pros: Once you are logged in to the website, the website will take you through the basic setup of your account, client's account, etc. Then you will be able to start using NinjaOne and easily go to the settings page to further customize your work.I consider NinjaOne as my right hand, NinjaOne is distinguished with a distinctive interface and a reasonable price with its dependence on cloud storage, and the software installations and server restarts are some of our best uses, and I'll not forget to mention that NinjaOne has a great email alerts system.A good advantage to work while ensuring social distancing to reduce the chances of infection with corona
cons: To fully work with NinjaOne and serve the customer will, I have to use software like TeamViewer or Splashtop. , which I find underestimated the degree of safety.
